title: symphyotrichum campestre
intro: Symphyotrichum campestre, also known as prairie dock, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves and yellow or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The medicinal properties of Symphyotrichum campestre are also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to aid in respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of conditions, from minor skin irritations to more serious health concerns.
